message:
feat(rchd): implement bounded remediation pipeline with TERM/KILL escalation and auditing (bd-vvmd.5.3)

Adds ProcessRemediationPipeline to rchd with:
- Bounded escalation ladder (observe -> TERM -> KILL) with configurable grace periods
- Policy evaluation via process-triage contract (allowlist/denylist, confidence gates, retry budgets)
- Per-action audit events emitted via EventBus with full evidence trail
- Safety guardrails: protected process blocking, concurrent pipeline limits, per-worker cooldowns
- Abort/rollback when confidence drops or safety checks fail
- Worker state tracking (action counts, hard terminations, consecutive failures)
- 20 integration tests covering all acceptance criteria

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -422,7 +422,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","title":"Define repo_updater adapter contract (commands, JSON schema, errors)","description":"Design stable integration boundary with repo_updater/ru: required commands, expected JSON outputs, failure taxonomy, timeout/retry behavior, and idempotency guarantees. Include version compatibility strategy and fallback behavior if adapter unavailable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Adapter contract specifies command surface, JSON schema, timeout budgets, and retry semantics.
- Contract documents authentication, host validation, and trust boundaries for repo_updater interactions.
- Error mapping normalizes adapter failures into RCH taxonomy with remediation guidance.
- Mockable adapter interface supports deterministic unit/integration tests.
- Contract includes explicit versioning and compatibility expectations for future updates.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:04.457858732Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T21:15:18.292823228Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T21:15:18.092230052Z","close_reason":"Completed: implemented repo_updater adapter contract module with schema/version/trust/fallback/error mapping, mock adapter interface, docs, and e2e contract test script.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contract","integration","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"],"comments":[{"id":215,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Paused while triage elevates bd-vvmd.5.1 as highest-impact actionable pick from bv --robot-next.","created_at":"2026-02-16T20:57:49Z"},{"id":217,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented in rch-common/src/repo_updater_contract.rs and wired via rch-common/src/lib.rs. Added docs/architecture/repo_updater_adapter_contract.md and tests/e2e/repo_updater_contract.sh. Validation run: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo clippy -p rch-common --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test -p rch-common repo_updater_contract_ -- --nocapture, tests/e2e/repo_updater_contract.sh. Note: workspace-wide clippy still has pre-existing blocker in rch/src/commands/daemon.rs (items_after_test_module).","created_at":"2026-02-16T21:15:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","title":"Implement daemon RepoConvergence service (convex-hull repo set + drift detection)","description":"Create service that computes required repo hull for active builds, compares worker state, and drives convergence actions through the adapter. Must expose drift state and convergence outcomes for scheduling decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- RepoConvergence service computes and tracks required repo convex-hull state per scheduling context.
- Service maintains explicit drift states with deterministic transitions and hysteresis rules.
- Convergence decisions are bounded by time/attempt budgets to preserve fail-open behavior.
- Service emits structured state-change events for observability and operator tooling.
- Integration tests validate healthy convergence, partial drift, auth failures, and stale-state recovery.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:04.663944951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:35:05.704331106Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:35:05.704256056Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["convergence","daemon","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.599381364Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.397218582Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.484662133Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":149,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RepoConvergence service should expose deterministic states (ready, drifting, converging, failed, stale) with timestamps and reason codes. Keep convergence idempotent and bounded per worker to avoid starvation. Include explicit separation between policy decisions and execution so dry-run/status surfaces can use the same core logic.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:24Z"},{"id":222,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: wired additional repo_updater convergence command surface into runtime path in rch hook pre-sync flow. Added command-aware idempotency keys and execution sequencing: SyncDryRun (read-only preflight) -> SyncApply (mutating convergence) -> StatusNoFetch (post-apply snapshot), all with strict fail-open behavior on validation/transport/adapter errors. Added helper coverage tests: test_repo_updater_command_name_is_stable and test_build_repo_sync_idempotency_key_for_command_distinguishes_commands. Validation performed: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace --all-targets; tests/e2e/path_dependency_fixtures.sh; tests/e2e/repo_updater_contract.sh.","created_at":"2026-02-17T01:59:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","title":"Integrate pre-build repo presence/freshness checks into worker selection flow","description":"Before assigning a worker, ensure required repos exist and meet freshness criteria. If not, trigger bounded sync or select alternate worker; otherwise fallback local per policy. Acceptance: missing-repo failures are prevented proactively.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Worker selection preflight verifies required repos are present and sufficiently fresh before remote build start.
- Preflight consumes convergence state and evidence rather than implicit assumptions.
- Missing/stale repo outcomes trigger explicit local fallback with reason-coded diagnostics.
- Decision logs include repo-level evidence and final selection/fallback rationale.
- Tests validate selection behavior under healthy, drifting, and unknown convergence states.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:04.866291287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T05:29:21.416554233Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T05:29:21.416487789Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["preflight","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater","selection"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:26.254572590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.994130539Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.797213178Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:26.455098267Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-vvmd.3.4","title":"Add background periodic convergence and drift alerting","description":"Implement background reconcile loop that keeps worker repo hull warm and reports sustained drift. Must avoid interfering with active builds and respect rate limits/time budgets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Background convergence loop runs at controlled cadence with workload-aware throttling.
- Loop avoids interfering with active builds and respects configured safety budgets.
- Sustained drift emits actionable alerts with ranked remediation suggestions.
- Alert noise controls (debounce/hysteresis) prevent flapping false alarms.
- Integration tests cover steady-state, prolonged drift, and recovery-to-healthy transitions.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:05.067406758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:34:03.188105892Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alerts","maintenance","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:15.194944166Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-vvmd.3.4","title":"Add background periodic convergence and drift alerting","description":"Implement background reconcile loop that keeps worker repo hull warm and reports sustained drift. Must avoid interfering with active builds and respect rate limits/time budgets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Background convergence loop runs at controlled cadence with workload-aware throttling.
- Loop avoids interfering with active builds and respects configured safety budgets.
- Sustained drift emits actionable alerts with ranked remediation suggestions.
- Alert noise controls (debounce/hysteresis) prevent flapping false alarms.
- Integration tests cover steady-state, prolonged drift, and recovery-to-healthy transitions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:05.067406758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:54:25.860213469Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T06:54:25.860147325Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alerts","maintenance","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:15.194944166Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.5","title":"Add operator commands: convergence status, dry-run, and repair","description":"Expose clear CLI/API surfaces for repo convergence visibility and manual remediation. Include machine-readable JSON mode and human-readable remediation guidance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Operator surface provides convergence status, dry-run simulation, and targeted repair actions.
- JSON output is stable and machine-parseable; human output includes concise remediation guidance.
- Repair operations are auditable, scope-constrained, and safe against untrusted paths.
- CLI paths preserve stdout/stderr mode guarantees and hook-safe behavior.
- Integration tests validate command behavior across healthy, drifting, and failed convergence states.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:05.269501534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:32:36.371436329Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T06:32:36.371363392Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","operations","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:15.394101401Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","title":"Add integration/E2E tests for repo convergence orchestration","description":"Validate repo convergence behavior end-to-end with deterministic fixtures and the shared reliability harness. Cover adapter parsing, drift detection, bounded convergence execution, and fail-open behavior under network/auth/staleness/partial-update faults.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scenarios cover nominal convergence, stale-repo repair, partial-update interruption, and adapter failure classes.
- Worker-selection decisions after convergence are asserted against expected readiness outcomes.
- Fail-open behavior is validated when convergence exceeds time budget or policy constraints.
- Logs capture convergence state transitions, selected remediation, and scheduling impact.
- Tests run via shared E2E harness with reproducible seeds and artifact retention.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:05.471705864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.899923941Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:26.656032500Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:19.206699348Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:19.410110788Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:26.849899079Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.899849291Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:13.525367699Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","title":"Add repo trust/allowlist policy for convergence scope","description":"Define and enforce an explicit allowlist policy for repos eligible for automatic convergence/sync. Prevent accidental sync of unrelated/sensitive paths and ensure convergence requests are constrained to approved roots under /data/projects. Include operator override workflow with audit trail.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Allowlist policy defaults to deny and restricts convergence to approved roots under `/data/projects`.
- Policy explicitly covers `/dp` alias handling and rejects traversal/out-of-scope targets.
- Override workflow requires explicit operator intent with durable audit trail metadata.
- Policy violations produce deterministic reason codes and non-ambiguous remediation guidance.
- Unit/integration tests validate allow, deny, override, and malformed-policy scenarios.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:25.040681503Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T00:39:11.513444614Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T00:39:11.513425297Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["policy","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:28.241160720Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:28.041951568Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":137,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rationale: repo convergence without an explicit trust boundary can unintentionally sync repos outside intended scope. This task establishes a security and governance guardrail for automated convergence.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:31Z"}]}
@@ -439,7 +439,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5","title":"Workstream: Stuck-Process Detection/Remediation via process_triage","description":"Purpose: address remote worker degradation caused by hung or orphaned compilation processes. Integrate `/data/projects/process_triage` to identify pathological process states, execute bounded remediation policies, and feed health signals back into worker selection so unstable nodes are deprioritized or quarantined.

## Success Criteria
- Detector/remediator pipeline achieves high precision with bounded, auditable actions.
- Periodic and on-demand triage loops are operational and exposed via status surfaces.
- Unresolved process debt influences worker scoring with stable recovery hysteresis.
- Process-triage tests verify safety boundaries, low false-positive behavior, and recovery outcomes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:01.024580281Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:16.366160512Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["processes","rch","rch-rel-2026q1","stability","workers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:26.054257607Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:22.503601592Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:22.702430032Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:16.366021632Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":134,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Safety principle: process remediation must be bounded and auditable. No broad kill patterns; only targeted actions derived from correlated build lifecycle evidence. Worker selection penalties reduce repeat incidents while preserving throughput.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:20:21Z"},{"id":145,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Process triage philosophy: prioritize precision over aggressiveness. Detection must correlate process state with known build lifecycle/ownership to avoid false positives. Remediation ladder is bounded (TERM then KILL where allowed), fully logged, and policy-gated. Remaining process debt feeds worker health penalties so degraded workers are deprioritized until stabilized.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","title":"Define process_triage integration contract and safe-action policy","description":"Specify how rchd invokes process_triage and interprets findings. Define strict allowlist/denylist and escalation ladder to avoid terminating legitimate workloads. Include rollback and observability requirements.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Contract specifies request/response schema, timeout/retry policy, and supported triage action classes.
- Safe-action policy defines explicit allowlist, denylist, and escalation thresholds.
- Contract includes audit requirements for every invoked triage/remediation action.
- Failure taxonomy distinguishes detector uncertainty from executor/runtime failures.
- Test plan covers contract conformance and policy enforcement edge cases.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:06.959266657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T19:33:02.351966388Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T19:33:02.351940199Z","close_reason":"Completed: contract/schema coverage and validation gates passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contract","process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1","safety"],"comments":[{"id":216,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Paused to execute current bv --robot-next top pick bd-vvmd.3.1; partial contract draft kept locally for later continuation.","created_at":"2026-02-16T21:05:55Z"},{"id":223,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: process_triage contract code in rch-common was previously not in the crate module graph (rch_common::e2e::mod did not declare/re-export process_triage), so contract/tests were effectively dormant. Wired module into rch-common e2e public surface ( + explicit re-exports), then fixed schema test resilience for schemars layout variants and resolved a clippy  finding in allow/deny conflict detection. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p rch-common process_triage_contract_ -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-02-17T02:02:46Z"},{"id":224,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction to previous comment (shell quoting artifact removed): process_triage contract code in rch-common was previously outside the active crate module graph because rch-common/src/e2e/mod.rs did not declare or re-export the process_triage module. Wired it into the public e2e surface via module declaration and explicit re-exports, then fixed schema-test robustness for schemars definition layouts and replaced the allow/deny overlap check with a clippy-compliant first-intersection lookup. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p rch-common process_triage_contract_ -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-02-17T02:02:53Z"},{"id":226,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Validation pass (2026-02-17): process_triage contract now includes explicit response schema coverage for required audit fields and failure-kind taxonomy; targeted and workspace checks all pass via rch offload. Commands: rch exec -- cargo fmt --check; rch exec -- cargo test -p rch-common process_triage_contract_ -- --nocapture; rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets; rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings.","created_at":"2026-02-17T19:32:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","title":"Implement stuck-process detector correlated with build lifecycle state","description":"Detect candidate stuck/orphan processes by correlating remote process state, timeouts, slot ownership, and build metadata. Goal: high precision detection with minimal false positives.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detector correlates process state, build ownership, slot allocation, and heartbeat/progress evidence.
- Confidence scoring differentiates slow-but-healthy jobs from likely orphan/hung processes.
- Detection output includes explainable evidence fields for operator review.
- Detector latency stays within configured triage budget under concurrent workloads.
- Unit/integration tests cover jitter, transient telemetry gaps, and false-positive suppression.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.166793804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T20:31:29.721435734Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T20:31:29.721417169Z","close_reason":"Completed: multi-signal stuck detector now correlates process liveness, slot ownership, build age, and heartbeat/progress evidence with operator-visible detector snapshots","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["daemon","detection","process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:16.608936776Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:15.940719864Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":151,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Detection quality bar: never classify by runtime alone. Correlate PID trees with build ownership, slot lifecycle, command fingerprints, and timeout envelopes. Model should support confidence levels so remediation policy can require higher confidence for destructive actions.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:31Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","title":"Implement bounded remediation pipeline (TERM/KILL escalation with auditing)","description":"Add remediation executor with conservative escalation, bounded retries, and full audit logging. Must never apply broad destructive process actions and must preserve system safety invariants.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Remediation pipeline enforces bounded escalation (observe -> TERM -> KILL) with explicit guardrails.
- Actions are scoped to verified offender processes only; no broad kill patterns are permitted.
- Every action emits audit record with evidence, rationale, actor, and outcome.
- Pipeline supports abort/rollback behavior when confidence drops or safety checks fail.
- Integration tests validate escalation correctness, safety boundaries, and audit completeness.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.370040536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T20:32:07.348572837Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1","remediation","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:29.723547694Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:16.809138818Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency tightened: remediation executor now explicitly depends on integration safety contract (bd-vvmd.5.1) to prevent implementation drift between policy and action pipeline.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:14Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","title":"Implement bounded remediation pipeline (TERM/KILL escalation with auditing)","description":"Add remediation executor with conservative escalation, bounded retries, and full audit logging. Must never apply broad destructive process actions and must preserve system safety invariants.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Remediation pipeline enforces bounded escalation (observe -> TERM -> KILL) with explicit guardrails.
- Actions are scoped to verified offender processes only; no broad kill patterns are permitted.
- Every action emits audit record with evidence, rationale, actor, and outcome.
- Pipeline supports abort/rollback behavior when confidence drops or safety checks fail.
- Integration tests validate escalation correctness, safety boundaries, and audit completeness.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.370040536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T07:44:00.274842057Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T07:44:00.274822962Z","close_reason":"Implemented bounded remediation pipeline with TERM/KILL escalation and auditing. 20 tests, 0 clippy warnings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1","remediation","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:29.723547694Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:16.809138818Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency tightened: remediation executor now explicitly depends on integration safety contract (bd-vvmd.5.1) to prevent implementation drift between policy and action pipeline.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:14Z"},{"id":230,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed by RainyHorse. Starting bounded remediation pipeline implementation.","created_at":"2026-02-18T07:16:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5.4","title":"Add periodic triage loop + on-demand operator triage command","description":"Provide scheduled triage and manual trigger paths, with configurable cadence and time budgets. Expose triage outcomes in machine-readable status output and human remediation hints.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Periodic loop cadence is configurable and does not starve active scheduling paths.
- On-demand command supports scoped triage runs with clear machine/human output modes.
- Loop and command share deterministic decision pipeline to avoid divergent behavior.
- Time-budget exhaustion is surfaced explicitly with partial-result semantics.
- Tests verify scheduling cadence, manual trigger behavior, and output determinism.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.576921402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:44.189818174Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["maintenance","operations","process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:17.006389954Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","title":"Feed multi-signal reliability debt into worker health/selection penalties","description":"Incorporate unresolved reliability debt into worker score/eligibility so degraded nodes are not repeatedly selected. Expand from process-only penalties to a unified multi-signal health model that combines process-triage debt, repo-convergence confidence, disk-pressure admission risk, and heartbeat/liveness evidence. Include quarantine/recovery hysteresis so recovered workers re-enter service safely without flapping.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Selection model aggregates multi-signal reliability debt (process, convergence, disk, heartbeat) into deterministic health states and penalties.
- Health states include at least healthy, degraded, quarantined, and probing-recovery with explicit transition rules.
- Penalties and quarantine windows are bounded and decay over time to avoid permanent exclusion from transient incidents.
- If all workers are unsafe/degraded, scheduler preserves fail-open local fallback with explicit reason-coded diagnostics.
- Unit/integration/E2E tests validate mixed-signal failure combinations, flapping resistance, and smooth reintegration after recovery, with structured decision logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.777582653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:05:01.400104Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["health","process_triage","quarantine","rch-rel-2026q1","reliability","selection"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:12.973954592Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:13.174224679Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:17.205757824Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:17.405356225Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:13.373597276Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":179,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Optimization revision: this bead now enforces a unified worker-health controller from multiple reliability signals (not just process debt), reducing repeated failed remote attempts and improving user-perceived stability under partial fleet degradation.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","title":"Add tests for detection precision and remediation safety boundaries","description":"Add test coverage for stuck-process detection precision and remediation safety boundaries across unit/integration/E2E levels. Distinguish benign long-running workloads from pathological orphaned/hung process states and verify bounded remediation behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover detector confidence scoring and false-positive protections.
- Integration tests cover remediation ladder behavior with auditing and guardrails.
- E2E tests validate triage + scheduler-penalty interactions on degraded workers.
- Test logs include process evidence inputs, classification confidence, chosen action, and post-action worker health.
- Acceptance target is deterministic decisioning with low false positives and no unsafe kill patterns.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.983669413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:48.503837260Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1","safety","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1yt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:48.503771917Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:29.926511645Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:20.435984999Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:20.637966592Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:16.155522943Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:13.951011737Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":192,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency tightened: tests now explicitly require bounded-remediation implementation (bd-vvmd.5.3) so safety assertions validate the real executor behavior, not stubs.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:14Z"}]}
